title: 15.-Contract No. 084534-1 Amendment:  NextGen Health Care Information Systems Inc. for Electronic Health Records

sections.recommendation:
Adopt a Resolution authorizing the City Manager to amend Contract No. 084534-1 with
NextGen Healthcare Information Systems, Inc. (“NextGen”), increasing the amount by
$191,136 for a total contract value not to exceed $1,010,472.20 and extending the term
from September 30, 2010 through June 30, 2026.
sections.fiscal_impacts:
Funds for additional professional services and software maintenance, in the amount of
$191,136, will be available in the Department of Health, Housing, and Community
Services’ FY2025 and FY2026 Realignment Fund budget as itemized below, and this
contract amendment has been entered into the Citywide contract database and assigned
contract number 084534-1. Spending for this contract in future fiscal years will be subject
to Council approval of the proposed citywide budget and annual appropriation ordinances.

sections.current_situation:
The Public Health Division utilizes the HIPAA-compliant NextGen Practice Management
(“PM”) and Electronic Health Record (“EHR") System to manage secure patient health
information and to streamline related business processes, including integrated electronic
claiming and remittance files. It satisfies the requirements for achieving EHR Meaningful
Use measures, and sets the stage to qualify for further American Recovery and
Reinvestment Act (ARRA) incentive dollars through the State. This software is critical to
the effective operations of the Berkeley High School (“BHS”) Health center and requires an
extension of maintenance and support. Staff seek spending authority with NextGen for an
additional year.
The NextGen PM and EHR software align with the City’s Strategic Plan Priority Projects,
advancing the City’s goal to be a customer-focused organization that provides excellent,
timely, easily-accessible service and information to the community.
sections.background:
In September 2010, City Council approved a contract with NextGen to implement the PM
and EHR system.
On June 28, 2011, Council approved a contract amendment with NextGen for data
conversion and additional training and implementation support.
In August 2011, the City implemented the NextGen Practice Management (PM) Module,
including electronic billing, at three Berkeley Public Health Clinics.
In June 2013, the City implemented NextGen’s Electronic Health Record (EHR).
On July 2, 2013, Council approved a contract amendment with NextGen for an
Immunization Registry interface, for additional software licensing, and for three additional
years of maintenance services (through FY 2016).
On July 14, 2015, City Council approved a contract amendment with NextGen for the
Patient Portal and for two additional years of software licensing and maintenance services
(through FY 2018).
On June 26, 2018, City Council approved a contract amendment with NextGen for two
additional years of software licensing and maintenance services.
On February 23, 2021, City Council approved a contract amendment with NextGen for
two additional years of software licensing and maintenance services.
Page 2
Page 3 of 4
Internal
Contract No. 084534-1 Amendment: NextGen CONSENT CALENDAR
May 20, 2025
On September 13, 2022, City Council approved a contract amendment with NextGen for
two additional years of software licensing and maintenance services.
ENVIRONMENTAL SUSTAINABILITY AND CLIMATE IMPACTS
The system replaces paper-based processes with secure online tools, which reduces
paper consumption, mailings, and the need for public health patients to travel to clinics
for routine information. In addition, the system supports electronic data exchange with
other healthcare providers and produces electronic data for claims and grant reporting.
The system operates on a virtual infrastructure thus reducing the City’s carbon footprint.
sections.rationale:
NextGen is the current system being used by city staff. The system supports clinics in
compliance with the Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act (“HIPAA”), which
outlines the privacy and security standards for health patient information. Staff
recommend maintaining support with NextGen services for updates, patches and
technical support.
sections.alternatives:
Staff considered implementing and maintaining the PM and EHR system without vendor
services, however, the risk of maintaining such a complex application without specialized
support is too great, especially given the impact on public health and BHS Health Center
services and workflow.
CONTACT PERSON
Kevin Fong, Director, Information Technology, 510-981-6541
sections.attachments:
1: Resolution
Page 3
Page 4 of 4
Internal
RESOLUTION NO. ##,###-N.S.
CONTRACT NO. 084534-1 AMENDMENT: NEXTGEN HEALTH CARE
INFORMATION SYSTEMS INC. (“NEXTGEN”) FOR ELECTRONIC HEALTH
RECORDS
WHEREAS, the NextGen Practice Management (“PM”) and Electronic Health Record
(“EHR”) System has provided a reliable and HIPAA-compliant system for collecting health
patient information; and
WHEREAS, the NextGen PM and EHR system has improved the efficiency of public
health clinic operations and resulted in increased revenue; and
WHEREAS, the NextGen PM and EHR system is a CMS-certified Electronic Health
Record technology, meets the requirements for achieving EHR Meaningful Use
measures; and
WHEREAS, a software maintenance and support agreement with NextGen is necessary
for system updates; and
WHEREAS, additional funding in the amount of $191,136 is available in the Department
of Health, Housing, and Community Services’ FY2025 and 2026 Realignment Fund
budget, and this contract amendment has been entered into the Citywide contract
database and assigned contract number 084534-1, and spending for this contract in
future fiscal years will be subject to Council approval of the proposed citywide budget and
annual appropriation ordinances.
NOW THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED by the Council of the City of Berkeley that the
City Manager is authorized to execute an amendment to Contract No. 084534-1 with
NextGen, increasing the amount by $191,136 for a total contract value not to exceed
$1,010,472.20 and extend the term from September 30, 2010 through June 30, 2026.
